steps.title: Use the roots
steps.content: If the two integer solutions are $m$ and $n$, then the quadratic can be written using those roots. For $x^2-ax+18=0$, the roots must have product $18$ and sum $a$.

steps.title: List factor sums
steps.content:
Use positive factor pairs because $a>0$. Their sums are
$$1+18=19$$
$$2+9=11$$
$$3+6=9$$

steps.title: State possible values
steps.content: The possible values are $9$, $11$, and $19$. The comma-separated entry is not the number $91119$. The answer is 9,11,19.
